skill.name: design-style-skill
skill.description: Select a consistent visual design system for PPT slides using radius and spacing recipes, component mappings, typography rules, and style mixing guidance.
skill.summary: Choose consistent PPT visual styles with practical radius, spacing, and typography rules.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but manual review found markdown and frontmatter false positives in SKILL.md. No prompt injection, network access, filesystem access, secret access, or executable workflow evidence was found. The skill is low risk and safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Interpreted as Command Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are fenced JavaScript examples and style snippets. They show PptxGenJS radius values and do not execute commands, invoke a shell, or accept user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are static markdown examples, not runnable skill code. No shell command, Ruby backtick expression, or execution path is present at those locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Frontmatter Interpreted as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm matches are in the YAML frontmatter name and description. The text describes PPT style recipes and contains no cryptographic API, hash function, encryption logic, or security-sensitive operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain only metadata and natural-language design guidance. There is no code or cryptographic behavior to exploit.

content.value_statement: Presentation teams often struggle to keep radius, spacing, and typography consistent across slides. This skill provides practical PPT style recipes that help Claude, Codex, and Claude Code apply a coherent visual system.
actual_capabilities: Provides four PPT style recipes: Sharp, Soft, Rounded, and Pill.
Maps corner radius values to buttons, cards, image containers, badges, and avatars.
Recommends spacing values for slides, cards, lists, groups, and major sections.
Defines typography ranges for notes, body text, subtitles, titles, and large metrics.
Guides style selection by presentation type, including data reports, business updates, and product decks.
limitations: Does not generate PowerPoint files by itself.
Does not provide full color palettes, image assets, or brand systems.
Uses 10 by 5.625 inch PPT slide assumptions.
Focuses on visual style rules, not narrative structure or content writing.

use_cases.title: Standardize a Business Deck
use_cases.description: Apply Soft and Balanced spacing, radius, and typography rules across a corporate presentation.

use_cases.title: Design a Product Launch Deck
use_cases.description: Choose Rounded or Pill styles for modern product slides with more whitespace and softer components.

use_cases.title: Tune Dense Data Slides
use_cases.description: Use Sharp and Compact guidance to fit tables, metrics, and charts without losing readability.

prompt_templates.title: Pick a Style
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner selection for a new deck.
prompt_templates.prompt: Choose the best PPT design style for a business update deck. Explain the radius, spacing, and typography choices.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Component Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the Soft and Balanced style to slide cards, buttons, badges, and image containers. Give specific radius and spacing values.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt for Data Density
prompt_templates.prompt: Review a data-heavy slide layout and adjust the style system for compact spacing, readable typography, and consistent component radius.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Mixed Style System
prompt_templates.prompt: Define a mixed PPT style system for a product launch deck with dense metric slides and airy brand slides. Include rules for transitions between styles.

output_examples.input: I need a professional quarterly business review deck.
output_examples.output: Use Soft and Balanced as the base style. Set medium radius for cards, standard page margins, and title sizes in the recommended business range.

output_examples.input: I need a launch deck that feels modern and spacious.
output: Use Rounded and Spacious for main content slides.
Use Pill and Airy for cover, agenda, and closing slides.
Keep large section spacing and avoid dense tables on brand-focused slides.

output_examples.input: My dashboard slide has many numbers and labels.
output_examples.output: Use Sharp and Compact rules for tighter spacing. Keep small radius values and reserve large typography for the key metric only.

best_practices: Choose one primary style recipe before designing slide components.
Keep outer container radius equal to or larger than inner component radius.
Match spacing density to content density before adjusting decorative details.
anti_patterns: Do not mix Pill buttons with very sharp containers without a clear reason.
Do not use large whitespace rules on dense financial or table-heavy slides.
Do not ignore the slide size assumptions when converting values to another format.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps choose and apply consistent radius, spacing, and typography rules for PPT slide design.

faq.question: Can it create a full PowerPoint file?
faq.answer: No. It provides design guidance that another PPT generation workflow can use.

faq.question: Which style is best for business presentations?
faq.answer: Soft and Balanced is usually best for corporate updates, business reviews, and general professional decks.

faq.question: Which style is best for data-heavy slides?
faq.answer: Sharp and Compact is best for dense tables, dashboards, and professional reports.

faq.question: Does it include color guidance?
faq.answer: No. The skill focuses on radius, spacing, typography, and component style mapping.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this with PptxGenJS?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance includes PptxGenJS-friendly values for slide components and layout spacing.
